title: Linux LVM and Storage Management
description: Manage logical volumes, inspect disk usage, mount filesystems, and work with block devices using LVM and standard storage tools.

tasks.prompt: List all block devices in a tree format showing partitions and mount points.
tasks.explanation: 'lsblk' displays block devices, their partitions, sizes, and mount points in an easy-to-read tree.

tasks.prompt: Show filesystem disk space usage in human-readable format.
tasks.explanation: 'df -h' reports total, used, and available space on mounted filesystems with KB/MB/GB units.

tasks.prompt: Show the total disk usage of the directory '/var/log' in human-readable format.
tasks.hint: Use du with summary and human flags: 'du -sh /var/log'
tasks.explanation: 'du -sh' summarizes directory size. '-s' gives a total only, and '-h' uses readable units.

tasks.prompt: Initialize '/dev/sdb' as an LVM physical volume.
tasks.explanation: 'pvcreate' marks a disk or partition for use by LVM, creating a physical volume.

tasks.prompt: Create a volume group named 'vg_data' using physical volume '/dev/sdb'.
tasks.explanation: 'vgcreate' combines one or more physical volumes into a storage pool called a volume group.

tasks.prompt: Create a 10 GB logical volume named 'lv_apps' in volume group 'vg_data'.
tasks.hint: Use lvcreate with size and name: 'sudo lvcreate -L 10G -n lv_apps vg_data'
tasks.explanation: 'lvcreate' allocates space from a volume group to create a logical volume usable like a disk partition.
'lvextend -L +5G' grows the logical volume by 5 GB. The filesystem must also be resized separately.

tasks.prompt: Resize the ext4 filesystem on '/dev/vg_data/lv_apps' to fill the logical volume.
tasks.explanation: 'resize2fs' expands an ext2/ext3/ext4 filesystem to use all available space in its underlying device.
'mount' attaches a filesystem to a directory, making its contents accessible at that path.

tasks.prompt: Display a summary of all LVM physical volumes on the system.
tasks.explanation: 'pvs' provides a concise overview of physical volumes, their volume groups, and free space.
